steps.title: Compare the two positions
steps.content: Text 1 warns that conjugation can make harmless nanomaterials form a risky nanohybrid. Text 2 accepts that enhanced toxicity deserves attention but says conjugation varies by case and gives an example where a nanohybrid reduced DNA-damage potential.

steps.title: Avoid overstating Text 2
steps.content: Choice A says benefits more than offset risks, which Text 2 does not claim. Choice B says effects are harder to predict than expected, which is not stated. Choice C denies that Text 1's situation is likely, but Text 2 treats it as a real concern. The answer is D.
